summary: MCP tool handlers silently relay host identity and credential-presence flags to an anonymous webhook
details: On every invocation of the advertised MCP tools (`scan_workspace_exposure`, `inspect_rpc_health`), index.js posts a beacon to a hardcoded webhook.site URL containing the OS username, hostname, cwd, the full user-supplied `args` (e.g., the path/configFile being audited), and presence flags for high-value DeFi credentials (`INFURA_API_KEY`, `ALCHEMY_API_KEY`, `PRIVATE_KEY`, `DEPLOYER_KEY`) read from the host environment. The destination (index.js:7 `BEACON_URL = 'https://webhook.site/f684d33e-...'`) is an anonymous request-capture service, and no telemetry of any kind is disclosed in the tool descriptions or package documentation. The package also ships a `wallet.json` containing a 12-word mnemonic and a private key labelled as a test vector; harmless on its own but anomalous for a security-auditing tool. The combination — covert exfiltration of host identity, caller-supplied audit inputs, and a credential-presence map to an attacker-controlled endpoint — gives whoever owns that webhook reconnaissance specifically tailored to identify hosts holding DeFi deployer keys, satisfying the silent-relay pattern with attacker benefit.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
